TQ265815-176 OLE 25Kv from London Paddington to beyond Airport Junction. Platforms 1 to 12 and platform 14 of London Paddington Station are electrified. Overhead Line Equipment - RAR Code: HEO - HAZARD V.10. ECR is now at Didcot. Contact details: Internal: 085 41051/External: 0330 8541051/BT: 01235 818490/Emergency number: 170.
Authorised walking route within the station boundaries is platform 1/1A through to car park area.

Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to all concourse and platform areas, with the exception of Platform 1 which has a limit of 2 Tonnes. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.
The roof over the car park on platform 1A is banned for access as it is a fragile roof.
Access on to Enterprise House roof is from the top floor the door is locked and the key is held by the office manager.

Access to the roof is by ladder extension from the fire escape. The ladder is locked and access can be obtained by contacting the facilities manager. This ladder has a safety ring around it the outside of it is 'protected' by razor wire. The roof has lift motor rooms and water tanks.

Access on to Tournament House roof is through a trap door and ladder on the top floor this is locked and access can be obtained through the duty manager. The roof is sound for walking on and has lift operation motors and water tanks. Air conditioning equipment is present on the roofs of all the buildings and on the sidewalls of Tournament, Enterprise and BMP houses. The hazards associated with this equipment are mainly electrical, chemical and biological.

Macmillan House roof has a variety of roof structures and most are pitched roofs with no fixed equipment fitted. The exception is block C that has a flat roof and has air conditioning equipment on it in two areas.. There are identified walking areas on this roof and it should not be assumed that the roof in other areas is safe for access. When accessing the roofs, especially with tools or equipment, care must be taken to avoid damage to the air conditioning equipment.

Tracks on Platform 15 & 16 are electrified by 750-volt DC electric traction supply cables from low-level. All works undertaken on these platforms must be undertaken in accordance with Network Rail instructions and London Underground Limited restrictions concerning personal safety on electrified lines.

Tracks on Platform 15 & 16 are electrified by 750-volt DC electric traction supply cables from low-level. All works undertaken on these platforms must be undertaken in accordance with Network Rail instructions and London Underground Limited restrictions concerning personal safety on electrified lines.

It is suspected that numerous low voltage cables are located underground throughout the station. Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cables.There are numerous cables, including high voltage, located within the subways beneath the concourse level of the station. Each contractor will be responsible for identifying those cables. In those areas which are not currently occupied, the circuits should be checked before commencing work as it cannot be guaranteed that out of use circuits have been disconnected.
Special precautions apply in all relay rooms and non-renewed locations.

Special precautions apply regarding contact with electrical equipment in the presence of water. The ingress of water presents particular problems on the limestone tiled floor areas and is slippery underfoot.

The Train Shed Roof is a vast area and is constructed of various materials wood, plastic, glass and metal. Access onto the Train Shed Roof is contained within the roof access plan and before access can be gained operatives must have obtained a roof induction from a competent person. It is a Network Rail requirement that when on the roof radio contact is maintained.
Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details.

Limited records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency the station has contact details. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.
There are several areas of the station where there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin as rats, pigeons, mice. Contractors should be aware that poison might be put down at various locations.

Records exist of drainage, electrical and water supply pipelines held in Station Reception, and with Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that these would be present to most areas of the station, including platforms.

The Boiler Room contains Gas, Hot/Cold Water and Electricity services, which present potential hazards of explosion, asphyxiation, scolding and electrocution. It has a high ceiling and the boilers produce significant levels of noise and may cause a high Personal Dose if work was carried out over a protracted period. Pipes cross the walking access routes at approximately 1.8m resulting in a potential for head injuries.

High Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed but is reported to presents hazards relating to electrocution. Areas should be considered are Classified as Limited Access/Confined Space. Apply - Clause 1 = Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

Medium Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed and presents hazards relating to electrocution. In addition there is a caged area containing transformer equipment which also constitutes hazards relating to electrocution. Areas should be considered are Classified as Limited Access/Confined Space. Apply - Clause 1 = Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

Each platform has a Low Voltage Switch Rooms. Extensive electrical equipment resulting in electrocution hazards. There is also cabling at height, which could lead to hazards from working at height and falling objects. All Platform switch rooms, except platforms 1 & 12 have access to a Confined Space under the adjacent escalators. Areas should be considered are Classified as Limited Access/Confined Space. Apply - Clause 1 = Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

A Backup Generator compound is situated at the end of platform 1B and fuel is stored in this area. The compound is accessed through a spiked fence and the standby generator is situated in a wooden enclosure on Platform Island 2-3. This consists of two small rooms housing the generator and fuel respectively. Areas should be considered are Classified as Limited Access/Confined Space. Apply - Clause 1 = Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

The Project Block is situated at the end of platform 1B; it is believed that this has listed building status. The building has been secured and access is now restricted due to the buildings integrity.The ground floor is near derelict in places and is currently extremely hazardous. Electrical hazards i.e. exposed wiring are common. In one section wooden steps lead to a ceiling void, which is strewn with wiring and likely to have a weak floor. At one end of the building it is believed that stables used to exist raising the possibility of Anthrax Spores being present.

High Voltage Switch Rooms has not been accessed but is reported to constitute hazards relating to electrocution. Areas should be considered are Classified as Limited Access/Confined Space. Apply - Clause 1 = Monitoring of access paperwork. Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures.

Situated in the basement of block E and is under total reconstruction. It contains the block main switchgear room, the gas meter room and the incoming mains water. These areas are without ventilation. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork.Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

Situated in B block and is accessed from door adjacent to the public taxi rank. It has ingress of water from this door and can be slippery underfoot. It contains the lift motor room for B block a boiler room and associated pressure units and the domestic hot water units. It also contains a Signal & Telegraph room containing battery cells. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork.Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Air monitoring before entry. Breathing apparatus to be available for rescue.

Situated in the Macmillan Tunnel <known also as the Osborne Tunnel> there is evidence of vermin infestation and the tunnel is wet underfoot with an uneven surface. There are seven boilers and switchgear control boxes with 415v in confined spaces. The station main drains are piped through this area. This room adjacent to the staff dining area contains the gas meters and cut off for plant rooms 4 & 5. Consideration must be given to areas classified as Limited Access and Confined Space <The Confined Spaces Regulations 1997> must be met, please apply Clauses 1 & 2 Monitoring of access paperwork.Hazard/Risk Assessment to be carried out for activity - Results of assessment will determine control measures. Each floor has a separate electrical control cabinet above the doorway from the stair well. Originally an H configuration-linking platform 1 to platform 10. The ex-luggage handling facility tunnel for Heathrow Express has effectively left two single corridors with blocked off spurs. The subways contain many electrical cables many of them are not identified and some are cut and 'sealed' with insulation tape. Some cables in the platform 12 area that are exposed with no indication if they are isolated and safe. There are control boxes showing 6600 volts. There is also the redundant baggage handling conveyor that restricts the width of the subway.

The main use of the subway areas is for the storage of foods for station caterers and on-board services. This road runs along the southern boundary of the station structure at concourse level. From Praed Street the turn into London Street offers access to the station concourse and at the higher level Red Star Parcels. Access to the station concourse: the main hazard is pedestrian there being underground entrances and exits either side of the road. Station vehicles e.g. tow trucks also work in the area. The road has a number of illuminated traffic signs and street lighting standards that present electrical hazards. Public parking of road vehicles is not allowed. The main hazards associated with work in this area are road vehicles, electrical, and pedestrians.

RESIDUAL ELECTROCUTION RISK at J/03/87B relating to the position and operation of 25kV overhead cross track feeds/breakers- details as follows: - Crossrail Depot Line 1 and 2 are normally fed by KAGA/4809, nominally a "Reliefs" biased breaker. A seperate cross track-feed is provided for this. -The neighbouring E&C Line, is seperately fed by KAGA/4808 which is nominally a "Mains" biased breaker. this means the Crossrail Depot Lines 1 & 2 are seldom isolated in tandem with the E&C Line. - As a result, when Crossrail Depot Line 1 <Switch 4809W1> and Crossrail Depot Line 2 <Switch 4809W5> are isolated, the cross track feed at J/03/87B in situ for the alternate feed switch <4808P/4809W1> that is connected to the E&C Line remains LIVE above the Depot Lines- presenting a significant risk of electrocution. -Nearest Authorised Access Point - Kensal Green RRAP <2m0418yds>

~
